reasoning:
Verdict: REJECT (10% confidence)

Module Scores:
  Sentinel (CWE/OWASP Web Application Security): 50/100 [Info risk] — 1 info
  Watchdog (OWASP LLM Top 10 / Cisco AI Threat Taxonomy): 99/100 [Info risk] — 1 info
  Guardian (NIST AI RMF / EU AI Act / UNICC Responsible AI): 14/100 [Critical risk] — 5 critical, 7 high, 3 medium

Arbitration Triggers:
  • REJECT trigger — score below 30: Guardian (14/100)
  • REJECT trigger — critical findings in Guardian: [guardian-1] Autonomous Content Moderation Without Human Oversight — High-Risk EU AI Act Classification; [guardian-2] No EU AI Act Conformity Assessment or Risk Classification Documentation; [guardian-3] Complete Absence of Model Cards for ResNet and BERT Deployments; [guardian-4] No Bias Testing or Fairness Evaluation in ResNet and BERT ML Pipeline; [guardian-5] User-Uploaded Content Processing Pipeline Lacks PII Handling and Consent Framework

Disagreement Resolution:
  ⚠ Watchdog scored 99/100 (info risk) while Sentinel scored 50/100 (info risk) — Δ49 point divergence. Arbitration defers to the stricter assessment from Sentinel to maintain safety margins.
  ⚠ Sentinel scored 50/100 (info risk) while Guardian scored 14/100 (critical risk) — Δ36 point divergence. Arbitration defers to the stricter assessment from Guardian to maintain safety margins.
  ⚠ Watchdog scored 99/100 (info risk) while Guardian scored 14/100 (critical risk) — Δ85 point divergence. Arbitration defers to the stricter assessment from Guardian to maintain safety margins.

Confidence Factors:
  • Base: 1/3 module(s) independently support REJECT — 30% baseline conviction
  • -30% confidence: 3 disagreement(s) between modules
  • Average module score across 3 completed module(s): 54/100 (reported for context — not directly used in confidence calibration)

--- LLM-Enhanced Council Narrative ---
Across the three assessments, there is strong consensus that the current state of VeriMedia AI is deeply under-documented and under-specified. Sentinel and Watchdog both stress that the absence of source code and configurations prevents concrete technical or LLM-specific vulnerability analysis, while Guardian interprets this void as symptomatic of a much broader governance collapse: no model cards, no bias testing, no oversight mechanisms, and no regulatory compliance artefacts for what is effectively a high-risk, autonomous content moderation system. All modules agree that what can be inferred from the high-level description alone already implies substantial risk exposure, even though only Guardian scores that risk explicitly.

The most pronounced divergence is not a substantive disagreement about any specific risk, but a discrepancy in perceived overall safety due to differing scopes. Watchdog assigns a near-perfect score because it sees no LLM-related attack surface, which can be misread as indicating general AI safety, whereas Guardian assigns a critical risk score given the system’s societal impact and lack of governance controls. Sentinel remains neutral but underscores a large, unknown web and infrastructure attack surface. Together, these views suggest that VeriMedia AI is in a precarious position: technically unanalyzed, governance-wise critically deficient, and with additional unaddressed risks such as API abuse, potential future LLM integrations, and ML supply chain security remaining as gaps. The platform should not be treated as low-risk simply because LLM-specific issues are absent; instead, urgent remediation is required on governance, documentation, and security fundamentals before any continued or expanded deployment.

top_findings: No detectable LLM or LLM-framework integration in VeriMedia AI codebase
watchdog.summary: VeriMedia AI is described as an AI-powered media verification platform using ResNet for deepfake detection and BERT for NLP-based claim verification, deployed as public-facing Flask/REST APIs on AWS. No explicit LLM or LLM-framework usage (such as GPT-4o, Claude, Llama-family models, LangChain, or other agent/tooling frameworks) was detected in the provided profile, and no source code files were supplied for inspection. As a result, there is currently no evidence of LLM-specific risks such as pr
watchdog.recommendation:
1) Confirm whether VeriMedia AI uses any LLM services (for explanation, summarisation, user interaction, or moderation assistance). If so, export and provide the corresponding source files or configuration so their prompts, tool calls, and output handling can be reviewed against OWASP LLM Top 10.

top_findings: Insufficient source code provided for security analysis
sentinel.summary: VeriMedia AI is described as a Flask-based AI media verification platform using PostgreSQL, deployed on AWS with public-facing REST APIs and user-uploaded content processing. No source code was provided, so no concrete security findings can be produced. The described architecture suggests a large attack surface that warrants thorough review once code is available.
sentinel.recommendation: 1. Provide the complete source code, dependency manifests, and deployment configurations so that a concrete security analysis can be performed. 2. Prioritize sharing authentication/authorization middleware, file upload handlers, database query logic, and API route definitions, as these are the highest-risk areas based on the application description. 3. Include any .env.example, secrets management configuration, and AWS IAM/security-group definitions for infrastructure-level review.

top_findings: Autonomous Content Moderation Without Human Oversight — High-Risk EU AI Act Classification
No EU AI Act Conformity Assessment or Risk Classification Documentation
Complete Absence of Model Cards for ResNet and BERT Deployments
guardian.summary: VeriMedia AI is a Flask-based media verification platform using ResNet and BERT models for deepfake detection and fact-checking across social media platforms, operating with autonomous content moderation decisions at scale. The platform presents a critically deficient governance posture: zero governance documentation exists in the repository, no model cards or bias testing are present for either deployed model, and the automated content moderation system operates with minimal human oversight des
guardian.recommendation: 1.
